# How Was I Hacked?

> A free tool that helps crypto users understand how their wallet was most likely
> compromised, and teaches the security practices that prevent it from happening again.
> No wallet connection, no personal data required.

## What it does

- Walks you through ~15-20 questions about what happened and diagnoses the most likely
  attack vector (phishing, malicious token approvals, seed-phrase exposure, malware,
  fake apps, blind signing, and more).
- Explains clear, practical steps to recover and to protect yourself going forward.

## Key pages

- [Home / Diagnose](https://howwasmywallethacked.com/): Start the guided diagnostic.
- [Learn Best Practices](https://howwasmywallethacked.com/learn): Seed phrase security,
  recognizing scams, safe browsing, token approvals, verifying transactions before
  signing, hot/cold wallet model, multisig, and what to do after being hacked.

## Trusted references this tool points to

- Security Alliance Wallet Security framework
- SEAL 911 (volunteer security responders for active incidents)
- revoke.cash (review and revoke token approvals)
- Safe (multisig wallets)
- HackedWalletRecovery.com (self-serve recovery for compromised wallets)
